title: Reader Outcomes, Publisher Stewardship, and Service Charter · Go Semantics Case: Feed Go Acceptance Evidence
summary: A coalition asks for a blog aggregator but has not named its readers, publisher obligations, freshness promise, moderation boundary, accessibility needs, or evidence of usefulness. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go acceptance evidence increment in reader-centered feed-service charter. Learners progress through prediction, explic…
objectives: Require changed feeds, malformed input, stale validators, tenant isolation, shutdown, restore, and reader-task evidence.
Set keyboard, heading, status, reflow, non-color, text-alternative, and reading-order acceptance evidence for the reader surface.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Acceptance Evidence · Theory Feed Go Acceptance Evidence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 4918 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate a useful reader and publisher service charter on observable decisions and inclusive evidence; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, date, identity, content, or encoding case and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
